Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Lasse Lidstrom'S Demand For Production, Inspection And Copying Of Documents To Albertson And Davidson, Llp, Set One, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-29-2016 Continued To Aug-10-16 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ciaron O'Sullivan for Lasse Lidstrom. Michael Loch for Albertson & Davidson.

Mr. O'Sullivan read the tentative ruling, stated that what he filed is on the website, that it complies with the rules, that the courtesy copies were printed off of the court's website, that he has no explanation as to why it was different, if it was different, that the first couple of pages were the same as the declaration as it was background information, that there could have been a mix-up when the copies were made to deliver to the court, that he would like to see what the court has, cited another Law & Motion tentative ruling where non-compliance resulted in a continuance and that it was a draconian measure to deny this motion. Mr. Loch stated that they object to these proceedings, that Mr. O'Sullivan did not comply with the rule to notify opposing counsel by telephone or in person when contesting, stated that the papers do not comply and that they were unable to respond to them. Mr. O'Sullivan responded that he had given written notice, that they received the notice and have appeared, that email has been the communication practice between the two firms, that in regards to the non-compliance of the papers they did not raise the issue in their opposition and that they do not have a standing as to the courtesy copies. The Court stated that it is concerned that the briefing received was not uniform, that Mr. Loch has not seen the additional pages, that it is willing to continue the matter to a new date so that everything can be re-served, that in the meantime the parties are to meet and confer regarding what is missing and the next steps to resolution. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that he does not understand Mr. Loch's comment regarding the 5 pages, that the background information is 2 1/3 pages, that any defects can be ironed out, that in regards to the lack of response there have been previous motions where no copies were provided, that he can meet and confer with Mr. Loch on Friday, that on 7/13 there is an 850 petition being heard regarding the extraordinary fees paid without prior court order and that he will want a ruling prior to mediation. Mr. Loch responded that waiting for an order on the 850 petition could push things out 6-12 months, that a legal malpractice action has been filed against his firm by Mr. Lidstrom and that the actions go against any steps to resolution. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that the 850 issue is purely a legal issue, that it can be determined quickly, that everything else is irrelevant as to what we are here for today and that opposing counsel only agreed to mediation after the court suggested it. The Court encouraged the parties to have constructive dialogue, stated that it will prepare an order which will set a new date for the motion sometime in the end of July or beginning of August and that it will also set a date for a mandatory settlement conference. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that he is unavailable from 7/15/16 through 8/8/16. The Court stated that it will prepare the order and that the parties are to meet and confer before the close of business on July 1, 2016.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Boncato. Court Reporter: Shelli Eng, CSR #11397, 650-571-1473 (Reported).

Petition For Order That Personal Representative: 1.Pay Outstanding Attorney'S Fees; 2. Liquidate Real Property As Necessary To Pay Attorney'S Fees; And 3. Approve Extraordinary Fees
Off Calendar May-16-2016 Continued To Jul-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ciaron O'Sullivan & William Matheson for (executor) Lasse Lidstrom. Michael Loch (via court call) for (petitioner) Albertson & Davidson.

Mr. O'Sullivan stated that there are various pending motions, that they are asking for repayment of $193,000.00 taken prior to court approval, that the client paid in reliance to a memo prepared by his former attorney, that they have asked for the funds back, that counsel refuses, that that are in the process of litigating, that mediation is set for next month, that they are not ready for trial and that they want an order for repayment. Mr. Loch stated that there cannot be an order, that there has been no notice regarding the statements or arguments, that there is no way to respond to a letter, that this is not the time or place to handle this, that counsel needs to bring a motion or a petition, that they are not following procedures and that they hope to resolve the matter at mediation. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that in opposing counsels initial petition they state that they have been paid, that it is the law that the payment required prior court approval, that they filed a demurrer and proposed mediation at the outset but were rebuffed, that they have spent a lot of money, that the court has ample authority to say that it be repaid to the executor and that it is being wrongfully held. The Court confirmed the parties mediation date of 6/19/16. Mr. Loch stated that if the court is granting the request they argue that there is no petition, motion or notice before the court. The Court stated that it can be reset for after mediation or they can tee up the issue and file responses and that it prefers that they tee up and get the issue before the court. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that he was hoping to avoid the expense of another petition, that opposing counsel is petitioning for $400,000.00 in fees, that they have already received $193,000.00 in fees and that if they have to file a petition they want a shortened time period. Mr. Loch stated that they request for full notice with time to respond. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that there is also an issue of Alexandra Ward, that she is suing them for fees and that her cause of action should be against Albertson & Davidson. The Court stated that it will continue the petition for order that personal representative pay outstanding attorney's fees, etc., to July 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, that it will allow time for Mr. O'Sullivan to file his petition, and for an ex parte to advance the date if necessary.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Nancy Wong. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-24-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Matheson for Lasse Lidstrom (Petitioner).

Mr. Matheson stated that there was an issue with the bonding agency due to his Client living parttime in San Francisco and part time in Finland. In addition, he argued that the will appointed his Client as the Executor, that bond was waived in the will, that he was the sole beneficiary with the exception of $15,000.00 and that he was not expecting any creditors to come forward. Furthermore, Mr. Matheson asked that in the alternative his Client be permitted to open a blocked account. The Court stated that local rules required posting of the bond. The Court granted the petition (signing the order for the same in open court). A bond in the amount of $10,000.00 is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
